learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate an understanding of schools as workplaces and be familiar with the statutory, ethical and other requirements that govern the work of teachers in schools

Demonstrate the application of professional and disciplinary knowledge to engage with curriculum, pedagogy, assessment and reporting of student outcomes, and use this knowledge to design lesson plans and learning experiences.

Create inclusive, supportive and intellectually challenging educational environments that take account of challenging student behaviours, demonstrate a range of teaching strategies that engage all learners, and set appropriate learning goals for students of all abilities and from broad range of backgrounds.
Communicate effectively and confidentially with students, care givers and fellow professionals in a range of educational, social and cultural environments to support student engagement.

Demonstrate skills of critical reflection to evaluate student learning and assessment data, as well as to make informed decisions regarding student learning and required modifications to teaching practices.

Demonstrate skills of and purpose for critical reflection and engagement with APSTs, colleagues and mentors to identify professional learning needs and develop achievable plans for personal professional growth that will contribute to improved student learning. In doing so, effectively engage with constructive feedback through the stages of preservice teaching and beginning teaching.
Demonstrate clear, fluent, and coherent communication skills consistent with personal, professional and academic conventions.
Demonstrate all teaching and professional requirements of the professional experience placement.

assessments.task_description:
Students must read the Policies and Procedures for Professional Experience Placements document and respond to the statement declaration on Blackboard by the due date. Failure to do so will result in a withdrawal of the placement and the awarding of a grade of Fail for the course. 

APST: 7.1, 7.2

Statement: I have read, understood and will abide by, the 2026 Policies and Procedures for Professional Experience Placements.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Failure to submit assessment will result in placement being pulled/ failing of the course.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.due_date:
Professional Experience 3/08/2026 - 18/09/2026

Professional Experience Report - Due 2pm 25/09/2026

The interim placement report is to be upload to Blackboard by the end of Week 3 of placement.
assessments.task_description:
Students are required to successfully complete 35 days of a supervised and assessed professional experience block placement in an approved school setting. The placement will be arranged only by the School of Education.

All students are expected to have read and understood the policies and procedures for professional experience document and to abide by them. Students who fail to adhere to the Polices and Procedures will be in breach and may have their placement withdrawn.

The 35-day block will comprise observations, planning and teaching full lessons and sequences of lessons.

During the practicum, students are expected to demonstrate competence across the Australian Professional Standards for Teachers (APSTs).

To do this, students will demonstrate that they have undertaken the inherent requirements of the role of a teacher. This means that students at the very least, will be expected to:

  • Attend the school at least half an hour before and after school start and finish times to discuss that day's and subsequent days' expectations
  • Attend any meetings held during the time of the practicum, e.g. departmental or whole of staff meetings or any professional development meetings where the school approves attendance.
  • Participate in extracurricular activities, etc., available during the block.
  • Preservice teachers must sign an attendance record on each school day at the time of arrival at or departure from the allocated placement school.

APST: 1 - 7
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Late submission of assessment without an approved extension will result in a failing grade.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
The Graduate Teacher Performance Assessment (GTPA) is an integrated assessment that connects the academic program and the professional experience, and in so doing connects research, theory and practice. The GTPA is a single culminating authentic summative assessment designed to demonstrate preservice teachers’ ability to meet the Graduate Teacher Standards and to plan, teach, assess and reflect on their final-year professional practice. The GTPA requires preservice teachers to demonstrate a capacity to interpret student data and modify and transform their teaching practices to improve student learning. This involves the preservice teacher in adapting and strategising teaching practices to meet the specific learning needs of students across the full range of abilities.

The GTPA is assessed by teacher educators within the higher education institutions (HEI). It is complementary to, but separate from, the Professional Experience Report. 

The GTPA task will be assessed by your workshop tutor at university. 

Refer to the “GTPA Preservice Teacher booklet" document for more information on the task.

APST: 1-7
assessments.submission_guidelines: Please upload your submission using the Turnitin link on Blackboard
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: Late submission of assessment without approved extension request via myUQ system will result in a failing grade.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Applied professional practice: Simulation, critical reflection and graduate learning goals
assessments.category: Essay/ Critique, Reflection, Role play/ Simulation
assessments.task_description:
This assessment focuses on developing critical reflection and professional learning goals as essential components of effective teaching practice. Drawing on 80 days of professional experience, students reflect on their evolving knowledge, skills, and teaching philosophy, informed by prior placements, supervising teacher feedback, and the Graduate Teacher Performance Assessment.

You are required to complete Parts A – C as outlined below. You will be asked to identify five professional learning goals: 

  • Part A: One or two professional learning goals
  • Part B and C: Three or four professional learning goals


In Part A, you will be asked to review and annotate a previously developed unit plan from your GTPA submission, to explicitly incorporate the Personal and Social Capability (ACARA), supported by a 400-word critical reflection that justifies key adjustments and identifies relevant professional collaboration, culminating in one or two professional learning goals.

Part B involves a simulated parent–teacher interview, where you will demonstrate effective, empathetic communication about a student’s learning, wellbeing, and inclusion, using your annotated unit plan.

Note: The role play will not be directly assessed by your tutor. Instead, you will be asked to self-assess and critically evaluate your performance and demonstrate your understanding of effective communication with parents/carers through your written reflection in Part C.

In Part C, you will produce a 500–600 word critical reflection evaluating your communication skills, use of data, engagement with parents/carers, and alignment with the Australian Professional Standards for Teachers.

Across the task, you are required to engage with academic literature (8–10 references) to support analysis and synthesis.

1000 words

If you use AI and/or machine translation (MT) technologies in any way in the written component of this assessment task, you must provide specific details about how it was used. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute academic misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Guidelines about conventions for appropriate referencing and acknowledgement, including an example coversheet to provide details of use, are available here: Overview - AI tools for assignments - Library Guides at University of Queensland Library

However, Part B of this assessment task is designed to protect academic integrity and assess your understanding of course concepts. You will be expected to complete a simulation exercise in the workshop where you complete a simulated parent-teacher interview with a peer and demonstrate your understanding and learning from Part A of the task, without the use of AI or other materials or tools. Use of prohibited materials in this section of the task may be investigated and treated as academic misconduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You are required to participate in the simulated activity (Part B) in the workshops held in Weeks 12 and 13. The written component is to be submitted via the Turnitin link on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: Late submission of assessment without approved extension request via myUQ system will result in a failing grade.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
An introduction to the course, final professional experience and GTPA

  • Introduction to the course and the placement
  • Towards autonomous professional practice: Preparation for the professional experience, statutory obligations, professional responsibilities, with a discussion of the Final Professional Experience Recommendations report
  • Continue to understand the legislative, school and employing authority requirements and ethical principles and practices in the teaching and learning process
  • Requirements of the professional experience placement - the Final Professional Experience Recommendations report
  • An introduction to the Graduate Teacher Performance Assessment task (GTPA)

GTPA: Practice 1 - Planning

APST: 1.1-1.5, 2.1-2.6, 3.1- 3.4, 3.5 - 3.7


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L08

learning_activities.topic:
Using data and evidence to inform the teaching cycle

  • Using student assessment data to evaluate student learning and modify teaching practice
  • Types of data and evidence available and how to use these to inform planning, teaching and assessment
  • How student assessment data (systemic and class-based) - is used to evaluate student learning and modify teaching practice
  • Setting learning goals that provide achievable challenges for students of varying abilities and characteristics making links with curriculum requirements and knowledge of how students learn
  • The Graduate Teacher Performance Assessment (GTPA)- Practice 1

APST: 1.1-1.5, 2.1-2.6, 3.1- 3.4, 3.5 - 3.7

Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L05

learning_activities.topic:
Professional Experience Placement (PEx / Prac)

The 35-day block will comprise observations, planning and teaching full lessons and sequences of lessons.

During the practicum, you are expected to demonstrate competence across the Australian Professional Standards for Teachers (APSTs).

To do this, you will demonstrate that you have undertaken the inherent requirements of the role of a beginning teacher. This means that at the very least, you will be expected to:

  • Attend the school at least half an hour before and after school start and finish times to discuss that day's and subsequent days' expectations
  • Attend any meetings held during the time of the practicum, e.g. departmental or whole of staff meetings or any professional development meetings where the school approves attendance.
  • Participate in extracurricular activities, etc., available during the block.

APST: 1-7


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L08
